channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: GO BANKRUPT FOR ISRAEL!!!! GOYIM!!!!! DJT — 171 replies — 684.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
GO BANKRUPT FOR ISRAEL!!!! GOYIM!!!!! DJT

Open thread • Replies: 171 • Updated: 2026-03-09 17:19 UTC

OP

GO BANKRUPT FOR ISRAEL!!!!

GOYIM!!!!!

DJT

Early replies (first 3)

61954017
>>61954004
BIDENS OIL

61954029
>>61954004
that kid should have practiced his aim a bit more

61954034
>>61954004
I voted for this

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61957229
>>61954140
America will be fine for the most part its us in Europe and Koreans/japanese who are fucked unless everyone plans a coordinated attack on the US dollar and just mercy kill the united states experiment

61957233
>>61954938
slaughtered

61957235
>>61957200
refusing to take the L is not the same as winning

61957296
>>61957229
>America will be fine for the most part its us in Europe and Koreans/japanese
i say this as a europoor but i think we have a better shot than the US, but only really because i think the US is so incredibly badly mismanaged right now. biden admin could have probably done the same broad strokes as trump but accomplished them much more competently.

u guys will probably hate the comparison but it looks like ww2 pre-war german/italian shenanigans except now it's US that's impossible to work with due to retarded leadership. if you're a random country like canada or whatever, you're probably more likely to want to integrate more with EU than the US or china at this point. it's clear US has given up on soft power and is trying to just strong-arm countries directly (which doesn't really work if you're trying to do it to everyone at once)

>unless everyone plans a coordinated attack on the US dollar
pretty sure china is basically already trying to by not buying bonds anymore but idk what exactly it would take (getting rid of the petro dollar?)

61957307
>>61954970
If they had nukes kikemerica and israhell wouldn’t have boomed them and possibly caused another refugee crisis

61957310
>>61954004
What a grammatical mess, what an absolute retard.

61957320
oil is linked to the price of diesel
diesel is linked to the price of food
◇ food is linked to the price of McChickens
no one will ever vote republican again

61957326
>>61954867
>Destroying Israel is going to be the best thing for humanity on the long run, the cost is nothing in comparison.

FTFY

61957361
>>61954140
First, the orange pedo has never read a book, doesn't read period, and is too much of a narcissist and an idiot to ever go "hmm, maybe I don't understand this, time to listen to people who are smarter than I am". Second, people around him are all sycophants, retarded influencers, religious fanatics, or terminal cowards. Third, MAGA as a whole abhors the idea of expertise, humility, knowledge, accountability, and professionalism - all the things that are critical for statesmanship.

Buckle up, because this is only gonna get worse.

61957387
>>61956282
The petrodollar was a mistake euros are so pussy the last one who remotely had some balls was hitler but he and his leadership was retarded so they lost the war now were stuck with 43eTfPd+ crypokike peckerwood running the world. Its funny in way
Anyways, death to America

61957395
>>61955335
>our enemy by their own choice
Ah yes i remember we were forced to overthrow their government. We couldnt help it.

61957404
>>61956714
Just like Dem voters supported the war in Gaza, am I right? Oh wait, they fucking hated it and Biden's support for Israel killed his poll numbers.

Mindlessly supporting your dear leader is very much a republican trait nowadays, unfortunately.

61957408
>>61956054
>, western powers supported the islamic revolution to start with since it was the shah's who nationalised british-persian oil.
are you actually retarded? The shah was quite literally installed via a cia plot and it was mossadegh who nationalized the oil. The Shah was the wests ally because he gave them everything. The revolution was definitely about getting rid of cia/anglo influence.

61957420
>>61954004
Everything they've been doing for decades has been leading towards this insane plan to have a one world government controlled by the Sanhedrin from Israel.
They want to destroy the global economy, millions if not billions will starve, civil wars are going to break out everywhere globally.
This is what globalism has enabled.

61957425
>>61954918
i voted on crypto ONLY and orange man is literally 1mil x better than dems for this.

61957437
>>61954039
Yep It's been nice knowing them but it's time for them to go and protect Israel, the love of their life

61957442
USA forced regime change on Iran with all the bad consequences that followed, then gave Saddam the precursors for mustard gas and egged him on to go to war with Iran. 200k+ Iranians died in this war.
>but they just hate our freedumbs!
USA has become comically villanous, a bad shadow of its former self...

61957443
>>61954004
your short time pain for their long time gains goy!

61957454
>>61954029
it was staged

61957470
>>61954918
you lost tranny. i voted for this.

61957577
>>61954004
another day of the rich fucking the poors. and the poors cheer.

61958149
>>61956054
How is it possible for someone to be so brazenly incorrect about concrete historical facts? Is American media just feeding the cattle literal fake news at this point?

61958261
>>61954004
Why does he write like an absolute retard, can Americans explain this to me?

61958269
>>61956866
Sorry, brother, teenage love is the only true form of romantic love. Once you hit that age when the first question a female asks you is "what is your profession, it's over :)

61958280
Can't we gang up to wipe out israel and live in peace with islamist?

61958799
>>61957320
Republicans don't care about fiscal responsibility or prices being low, or anything else really. They just want someone to get up on the podium and be a mirror to their ideology, regardless of what the actual outcome is

61958816
>>61957454
This. It's all fake and gay. Israel literally owns Hollywood

61958832
>>61957296
China just has to do literally nothing in order to win against the US. Ideal world they continue to invest in renewables and infrastructure to help then rebound from global currency meltdowns, and subsequently would give them some isolation from oil price volatility as well. The US is having the equivalent of a brain hemmhorage, it's not long until all executive function is completely dissolved

61958966
>>61957296
>retarded leadership
more like retarded citizens
burgers are fucking golems who can't be trusted and will make this mistake again in the future

61959102
>>61954004
Thankfully, i am currently driving a company issued vehicle and fuel card for most of my driving.

item.title: WFH CHADS REPORT IN — 38 replies — 152.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
WFH CHADS REPORT IN

Open thread • Replies: 38 • Updated: 2026-03-09 17:24 UTC

OP

Let's all laugh at the wagies who have to pay insane amounts for gas to get to work kek

Early replies (first 3)

61953886
>>61953855
Imagine spending half your days pay worth of money just to get to work and back lmao

61954053
>>61953855
antisemitic wfh antizog antimiga chads will inherit the earth

61954077
>>61953886
>half
You mean full for minimum wage chads, don’t forget about rent and bills too.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61954356
>>61953855
Im currently clocked in on the job right now. Sundays is essentially my monday where i clean my apartment (rent is 780 per month). I also live a block away from corporate headquarters (even though I never go into the office). The thing is that I only go there for the 24/7 open snacks and sodas at 4am. During the daytime though before my shift starts at 3pm, i do my daily workout routine at the company provided gym 6 days a week. After doing my weightlifting routine and showering at the company spa, I treat myself to a double patty burger, bacon, cheese and 2 full cups of the soup of the day for a total of 6 dollars.

61954489
I WFH.

There was a very kino moment on Saturday morning where I woke up earlier than usual, it was sunny for the first time in weeks, I went jogging, did a few chores, then I drove to McDonald's at 10 am for a bacon and egg mcmuffin (never had McDonald's breakfast before) and a black coffee and it all felt so kino. It felt like being unemployed again.

61954507
>>61954234
Make your own lunch you lazy faggot

61954577
>>61954356
I also forgot to mention that the corporate office has a fully staffed kitchen team that cooks anything you request. If you want an omlette at 6pm, you can have it. If you want a burger at 8am, there you go. Including daily desserts and a soft serve machine next to the salad bar. (Sorry but i still wont sit in the cubicle. I refuse to do so!)

61954585
>>61953855
I'm an EVChud (we won btw) but I also work from home.

ICEissies big mad :)

61954808
>>61953855
WfH accountant reporting in
I hate working, need to find a new job in the next couple months because rn my work load is low but it's going to increase a lot

61954827
>>61954162
Build one yourself nigger. Use claude

61954912
>>61954577
Damn how big is that fucking office?
We just get our food paid for by the company (unless it is retardedly expensive, but they cover enough for the usual menu price) and eat at the restaurants around the office which are all pretty good (mediterranean country).
The office itself is very old-school and just has a canteen room for eating. No free stuff of cooking staff. The usual for a boomer era company.

61954933
>>61954808
Get a government job anon. In your field the pay difference isn't even that big afaik and the workload difference is huge.

61954954
I have been applying for WFH jobs with no success. Any advice?

I haven't applied for a new job in years and I am amazed at how many fake job postings there are. I had one company that wanted me to sign their t&cs and very an account to get an interview. Looking into it, they were going to our new through multiple technical tests for interviews and then keep my responses to train their AI

61954976
>>61954954
Oh and I have avoided Social media all these years and now finally caved and got LinkedIn because job listings actually want you to list your LinkedIn account on your application. Not sure if it'll actually help at all

61954991
>>61954912
Pretty big, the weightlifting room at our facility is comparable to an average high school basketball gym in size.

61955153
>>61954933
>Get a government job anon
I want one but none in my county have WfH, I mean I haven't gotten an interview with one yet so I'd have to ask

61957384
>have regular 9-5
>wake up an hour earlier for a work event
>6am
>out the window
>still night time
>traffic in full swing
There is something very wrong about this. The natural world is brutal but this is the same thing just in slow-mo suffering mode.

61957401
>>61953855
Bros, I am officially celebrating six years of getting away with WFH. Thanks Wuhan!

61958246
>>61957384
Even if it wasn't for the commute I couldn't go back into the office. Feels like a prison & I can't stand coworkers

61958290
>>61953855
Thank God for WFH. Happy Monday bros and when you're a WFH wagie, it actually is a happy Monday.

61958386
>>61957401
>>61958290
A toast to my WFH brothers, you wearing PJs this morning too?

61958439
Posting from in bed because I /madeit/ thanks to biz :3

61958532
>>61953855
Another day, another opportunity to work 0 hours and put 8 on my timesheet.
What do you guys do? I'm a consultant for a niche eng field. 3rd party contractor (hence timesheets to record hours per project).

61958544
>>61958246
I actually liked my coworkers and shootin the shit at the office, but since March of 2020 I've been 100% WFH and prefer this 10x more. Fuck driving in traffic.

61958574
>>61958386
I was wearing my standard comfy pants but have now slipped into my socially acceptable gear (jeans) for my daily walk with my dog. Cheers brother, enjoy this fine day.

61958588
>>61953855
WFH is true king status. Sometimes I fuck my wife on the clock. I've had her under my desk sucking my dick while on a teams meeting. So good.

61958609
>>61954954
Job market is brutally bad right now. That makes us folks who have good WFH jobs even bigger Chads. And yea, fake jobs out the ass, AI recruiters, AI interviews, fake shit all over.

61958767
i work from home everyday and i get paid well.
problem is that my apartment has become my prison. i have become more agoraphobic than over and depression is creeping in. my portfolio is doing good though.

61958794
>>61958767
Go take a walk for 15 minutes whenever you feel that. Always cures it for me

61959001
>>61953855
What field do you faggots work in where wfh jobs fall from the sky like rain?
And don't fucking say tech cuz every tech role near me is fully onsite

61959006
>>61958588
I remember fucking my gf on my lunchbreak once lol it was a good memory

61959021
>>61959001
If you have the skills, any job will allow you to work from home that doesn't have a specialized worksite (Lawyer::Courtroom, Soldier::Iran, Tradesman::Jobsite)

61959146
What WfH jobs are there where you basically don't do shit? Or if you have work, no one will know if it's completed or not

item.title: >last night oil futures were nearing 120 >reports of G7 will be releasing SPR >oil drops under 100 >later find out that… — 18 replies — 72.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
>last night oil futures were nearing 120 >reports of G7 will be releasing SPR >oil drops under 100 >later find out that…

Open thread • Replies: 18 • Updated: 2026-03-09 17:24 UTC

OP

>last night oil futures were nearing 120
>reports of G7 will be releasing SPR
>oil drops under 100
>later find out that they never planned on doing it


So they're just releasing fake news to make people panic sell?

Early replies (first 3)

61958573
>>61958562
What's petrol gonna cost per liter in Europe when oil is $250 a barrel?

61958593
>>61958562
Yes and no. The whole war is a psyop so that they can sell the oil reserves/venezuala raid oil at an insane markup before prices return to a more neutral stance

61958611
>>61958573
2 dollar per liter or 13 per gallon. US would probably be half but idk if modern society can operate with those costs? And if Iranians are destroying oil infrastructure, Arab states shutting down oil production which takes months to restart and ports are being bombed it might take long time for natural flow to restart. But idk why they lied about it though

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61958627
>>61958593
>l reserves/venezuala raid oil


Venezuela oil infrastructure is decrepit and can barely hit 1 million while Hormuz is 20 million per day. And that million will need investments and years to even match UAEs production per day.

61958703
>>61958627
The US has appropriated 80 million barrels of oil from venezuala since the new year. A clean 4 billion in additional value since the Iran conflict started, of course headed straight for private hands. We've only spent 100 billion in taxpayer money on Iran so far, a very efficient laundering ratio

61958725
>>61958703
>The US has appropriated 80 million barrels of oil from venezuala since the new year. A clean 4 billion in additional value since the Iran conflict started, of course headed straight for private hands.


And Hormuz makes 20 million barrels a day. So within this week we lost 160 millions of barrels heading into global oil market. Can Venezuela fulfill that gap for rest of global market? India , east Asia , South America and Europe? At best it can help stave off few cents off gallon in US not replace Hormuz.

61958760
>>61958562
what's the purpose of strategic reserves when you do not use them in a scenario like this? do they think russia will invade them right after they burned through them or something?

61958770
>>61958725
Fill the gap? I don't think you're reading what I'm saying. The US has reserve oil that it (read: private interests) wants to sell at a premium, and it has a convenient piñata that it can hit to make that happen in Iran. The oil price has been rock bottom for an extremely long time. The health of the global economy or whatever has not been, is not, and never will be the priority of the US government

61958797
>>61958562
Buy the dip

61958837
>>61958770
>The US has reserve oil that it (read: private interests) wants to sell at a premium, and it has a convenient piñata that it can hit to make that happen in Iran. The oil price has been rock bottom for an extremely long time. The health of the global economy or whatever has not been, is not, and never will be the priority of the US government


>East Asia hit by energy crisis
>electronic manufacturing needed for mass data center build up stalls or becomes increasingly expensive
>AI companies either get in more debt or halt production

So biggest growth sector so far has been tech and you're telling me they're willing to throw it all away for oil barons in Texas to sell expensive oil that will have little to no demand. Not only is oil scarcity bad enough Qatar makes helium used in semiconductor manufacturing so what will Taiwan use then to keep up with big demand for next gen chips.

61958856
>>61958562
That fake news cost me over 10 grand.

61958857
>>61958573
Some 'expert' here in Australia was saying that every $1USD per barrel that crude goes up we see an increase of $0.1AUD per litre at the pump.
Fuel was around $1.70 per litre before the war and is now between $2 and $2.20 so seems to be holding true so far, but if oil reached $250 a barrel I'm sure this math would break down. But it would be $3.50AUD per litre at an absolute minimum.
Fuel in Europe is usually more expensive than Australia, maybe due to taxes you guys impose or something. But $3.50AUD is €2.13 so don't expect to pay less than that if oil goes to $250.

61958935
>>61958837
Frankly, yes. If the US had actual planning capability the oil price would not have spiked, such is the privelege of being the world reserve currency. Israel is their greatest ally, and they can slide in some backdoor deals while they're executing their god-given mission to bomb iran. I'm not saying this is a good idea or anything, but this is what you do when you're a billionaire who wants more billions

61959019
>>61958935
Most of US economic growth is in AI for past few years. Most of these AI companies are owned and run by Jews like Ellison and Sam Altman. If they truly believed these things could become AGI and be smarter than any human combined they wouldn't have attacked Iran while risking the biggest private sector build up ever seen in US history. Plus Arab money is fueling this industry as well so not only are they hurting East Asian companies who make chips and parts needed but capital investment that keeps it going. Now we might see billions lost , companies default and bubble might pop if this goes on any longer.

61959081
>>61959019
What does that have to do with trump's pocketbook? Given the track record, he (using a proxy) almost certainly shorted the shit out of whatever dips on rising oil prices. In the end this whole thing is a stunt, it'll be back to normal in a week. Remember, nothing ever happens. This is all a game being played with peoples' lives

61959087
>>61959019
If Israel inadvertently kills the AI industry I will forgive them for everything, even 9/11.

61959119
>>61959081
Iran will just roll over after their leader was killed , oil refinery destroyed and navy obliterated? The new leader had his whole family wiped out
>>61959087
There's secular diaspora Jews and religious Jews who want messiah.

61959142
>>61958856
Has /biz/ never bought the top?

item.title: ECON EXPERTS GET IN HERE — 14 replies — 56.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
ECON EXPERTS GET IN HERE

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-03-09 17:28 UTC

OP

I see so much shilling from both sides of the aisle right now about how the economy will hold up, both in the United States and also around the world.
So, what is it? Will the global economy collapse? Will the United States economy collapse?
What can we expect from oil prices?
I was doom-posting when I saw the oil futures hit 118.
It already appears to be coming down rapidly.
I was honestly expecting this to be a SHTF situation. However, it appears I was wrong.
ITT, we state our opinions and argue whether it is so over or we are so back.

Early replies (first 3)

61958990
>itt brown third worldists cope about Iran being a leaderless paper tiger.
You honestly thought 68 iq goat rapists dictate our fucking energy policy?
You all bought at the top, again. Lol who am I kidding none of you have money let alone investments.
Kys

61959005
>>61958990
>OP: "I was honestly expecting this to be a SHTF situation. However, it appears I was wrong."
>Anon: YOU ARE A SHITSKIN WHO BOUGHT THE TOP!
>This anon doesn't know how to read.

61959012
>>61959005
You are brown!!!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61959033
>>61959012
Blah blah blah, nigger nigger nigger.
Now, denounce the Talmud and type the word "GOD" in your next post to prove you are not Jewish.

61959060
>>61958974
econ expert here
prices could go up or they could go down

61959073
>>61959060
TRVKE!
HOLY SHIT THIS ANON IS CORRECT!

61959099
>>61959033
God knows you are brown. Now never post on the Internet again.

61959113
>>61959099
He said God.
I noticed that you refused to deny the Talmud.
Curious...
>HE SUPPORTS THE SIDE THAT BURNED A DEMON! HE MUST BE BROWN!!!

61959134
>>61959113
Jews are brown, just like you, retard.

61959150
>>61959134
Continuously neglects to deny the Talmud.
Why are you so butthurt, anon?
I merely wanted to discuss the impact of crude futures on the U.S. and the world at large.

61959154
>>61958974
The rest of the brown world will be more negatively impacted than the USA and we (the USA) will continue accumulating the worlds wealth while you (brown) will seethe

61959161
>>61959154
HE DOESN'T SUPPORT (((CURRENT THING)))!!!
HE MUST BE BROWN! GET HIM!

61959163
>>61959150
Brother I hate jews because they are jews. You hate jews because you think they are white. And thats because you are brown.
>denounce the Talmud
Okay I denounce it.
Now you denounce Mohammed.

61959169
>>61959161
You are so brown.

item.title: HOW DO I PROFIT OF DEATH AND DESTRUCTION? — 13 replies — 52.0/hr

HOW DO I PROFIT OF DEATH AND DESTRUCTION?

Open thread • Replies: 13 • Updated: 2026-03-09 17:20 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

61955815

61955820
>>61955772
Pick a Chaos God

61955899
>>61955772
be american. they've continuously done this for more than 100 years now.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61956119
>>61955820
Bottom left is literally me.

61956147
>>61955899
I seriously believe we’ll all be held to account in the afterlife for this.

61956151
>>61955899
What’s the alternative, just let evil dictators massacre their people? The places where we didn’t intervene had the most deaths statistically speaking, like Maoist China.

61956172
>>61956151
Shalom Ari , how is the weather in Tel Aviv ?
> literally cause more incessant pain and suffering in every war since ww2 with nothing to show for it besides 48 trillion in debt .

61956186
>>61956151
You need to grow up someday and stop swallowing the propaganda. We have never intervened in the affairs of another country with altruistic intentions.

61956193
Palantir, Lockheed Martin, Raytheon, gold, the Tel Aviv stock market

61957536
>>61955772
Become a rabbi.

61957898
>>61955820
WHY IS KHORNE WIPING OUT MY FOLIO AAAAAAAAA

61959116
>>61955772
shut the fuck up you wannabe misanthrope.
laughable.

item.title: 2 years from now will you come back and say, 'This lil nigga Trump, I love him for giving me such cheap entries - $1200… — 6 replies — 24.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
2 years from now will you come back and say, 'This lil nigga Trump, I love him for giving me such cheap entries - $1200…

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-03-09 17:29 UTC

OP

2 years from now will you come back and say, 'This lil nigga Trump, I love him for giving me such cheap entries - $1200 ETH and $70k Bitcoin' or you will stlil hate him. What's the higher IQ behavior here?

Early replies (first 3)

>>61958976
Trump will save America.

61959066
>>61958976
This fookin' guy. 'ey I'm walkin' 'ere!

61959100

61959160

70k pedocoin a bargain

item.title: IRAN WARNS OF $200 OIL — 2 replies — 7.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
IRAN WARNS OF $200 OIL

Open thread • Replies: 2 • Updated: 2026-03-09 17:15 UTC

OP

An Iranian military spokesperson said oil could hit $200 a barrel if U.S. and Israeli strikes on its energy infrastructure continue. Recent attacks, including Israel hitting Tehran refinery storage tanks, forced Iran to cut fuel allowances. Since the conflict began, Iran and its proxies have targeted oil facilities in Saudi Arabia, Iraq, and Kuwait, reducing regional output.

Early replies (first 3)

61958258
>>61958124
the raped

61959071
>>61958124
>warns
Free money.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

No additional replies in the latest window.

item.title: The US has 10 days — 170 replies — 98.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
The US has 10 days

Open thread • Replies: 170 • Updated: 2026-03-09 15:47 UTC

OP

The US has 10 days to clear the strait of Hormuz or global markets will crash (not a hyperbole)

At first, markets brushed off the conflict as another 12 day war. However, realization is setting in that Iran is actually capable of deterring passage through the Hormuz strait by 90%.

The problem is that every day that goes on is the equivalent of shutting off 10+ billion barrels of oil every single day. As storage becomes full, producers are forced to shut in production, which takes 2 weeks to restart. Thankfully, most of the world is fine for 1-2 weeks without gulf oil. After that, problems begin.

Oil will reprice a geopolitical premium and $100 is LOW for the magnitude of production shutdown.

What happens: bond yields will roar to new highs as a huge wave of inflation begins. All rate cut probabilities get removed, and you will see the traditional end of the business cycle, high oil prices will turn growth into recession for most economies. None of this is priced in equities. And the longer it goes on, the higher likelihood of it happening.

How to end it? Trump has fallen for a Trump. There is no way to clear irans fast mountain ranges littered with AD and missile silos, thus tankers will keep getting shot in the Hormuz strait. The only way is to put troops on the ground.

Don’t really care if you think I’m a permabear or a doomer, I’m just giving you a warning that for whatever reason, equities don’t want to price in. They don’t understand that this is an existential war for Iran, it’s not some “geopolitical dip”.

Pic is Asian jet fuel prices

Early replies (first 3)

61938343
>>61938341
Slurp XOP? Wut do

61938344
So should I sell my TLT?

61938345
>>61938341
>I’m just giving you a warning that for whatever reason, equities don’t want to price in
Trump spent all of last year insider trading and TACOing so he's not trustworthy anymore

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61956110
>>61938402
Nah you can launch shaheads from the back of a ute, I've seen it done
Also, USVs do not require any launch platform at all and have proven devastatingly effective in the black sea - though with that said I have yet to see Iran or its houthi proxies deploy those in any serious number

61956116
>>61938341
just got into trading, is it supposed to be doing that?

61956309
>>61956116
yes. it's normal.

61956552
>>61956041
>meanwhile in Iran

61956572
>>61938341
two more weeks

61956577
how much time would you wager a release of circa 400 million barrels from the G7 would buy?

61956580
>>61956577
Consumption is ~100m/day desu

61956581
>>61956577

61956822

61957005

61957036

61957128

61957134
>>61957048

>hyperbole
not
>A hyperbole

you say "a hyperbole" and everyone knows you're from india

61957139
>>61956041

61957381
>>61942837
>il price going up means US gov will have more money to balance the budget
the us gov does not get anything from oil prices going up kek. Slightly higher taxs from oil companies but not in any siginificant amount.

61957411
>>61957134

I’m not from India I literally have a masters degree from an Ivy League. I don’t give a fuck what some loser thinks. Post your longs or get out of my thread. We already know you got burned on your weekend CL shorts

61957592
Frens, leave stonks.
Switch to bonds real quick.

61957796
>>61938345
As other anon said, wholy retard.
>Trump made US oil independent during first term, and US is top 3 oil producing country now.
>Oil price going up means US gov will have more money to balance the budget.

61957822
>>61938445
>one of the underlying objectives was to tank the economies of EU
EU economies are tanking and dying by themselves.
EU is collapsing, Germany, the largest economic EU powerhouse is having mass layoffs.

>. One way to onshore manufacturing and heavy industry is to be one of the few places with relatively cheap energy
Correct.
>WW3 scitzos
were wrong .
Why do we need ww3, if you want to restart manufacturing? They don't need a war.

61957823
>>61938341
Fake news! Democrat Hoax! Not confirmed by CENTCOM, plus it looks like AI. Be aware of malinformation.
MAGA!
Now is a good time to buy ETH

61957832
>>61938425
IF you don't understand geo politics, this doesn't mean Trump and his team don't.
It just means YOU don't understand it.

61957835
>>61957796
This anon gets it

61957837
>>61957832
This! MAGA!!

61957854
>>61938341
Rolling that this war goes on for years months and the global economy shits itself.

61958442
>>61957018
>a hyperbole
You're retarded. Hyperbole is a noun and you are too used to retarded internet speak that uses it as an adjective (which is hyperbolic).

Furthermore, dismissing a message because of grammatical or linguistic issues you find in the message is retarded. Even if "hyperbole" were an adjective, were you really unable to understand the meaning of his message? Language is about transacting messages/meaning. If the message was delivered, that is all that matters.

t. engineer working on semantic compression method for agent-to-agent communication

61958506
need some stock picks for slurpage. if the market crashes

61956565

61956566
>>61956532

61956635

61956651
>>61956635

61956656
>>61953183
What part of 'short term pain for long term gain' don't you retards understand? The big guy's got a plan. All this market noise is generated by libtards to make him look bad. I feel bad for you retards for thinking these squiggly lines on your screen are real and mean something.

61956675
>>61956566
that's what they're being told
and they will listen lol

61956683
>>61956532

61956915
>>61955845

61957031

61957087
>>61956566

It's like how people thought WW1 was going to last a couple months because there hadn't been a major war in Europe for a hundred years aside from the Prussians sealclubbing the Austrians and French when they were weak.

61957147
>>61956463

61957315
>>61956463

61957348
>>61953183

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=eVTXPUF4Oz4

61957485
>>61956463

61958040
>>61955248

61958585
>>61957087
It's nothing new. During the first battle of Bull Run civilians took picnic baskets to watch the war like it was a sports game then got trampled to death during the retreat. When a generation forgets the horrors of war this cycle just repeats over and over.

item.title: How would you breakout of the NEET cycle if you had too — 24 replies — 13.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
How would you breakout of the NEET cycle if you had too

Open thread • Replies: 24 • Updated: 2026-03-09 15:46 UTC

OP

How would you breakout of the NEET cycle if you had too

Early replies (first 3)

61956383
>>61956364
Stop dealing in the real life.

61956387
By cumming all over OP pic

61956426
>>61956364
I'd EET

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61956433
>>61956364
Work at a warehouse or get your security license and work security

61956436
Do an online degree and get a job. Use competency based education and pell grants

61956484
>>61956364
I am desperately looking for the answer. I guess driving a van and doing deliveries would be cool.

61956595
>>61956364
>work out
>apply for warehouse job
>see look i am big and strong hire me pls
>get hired and deal with ghetto retard coworkers
>deal with it for 1 year
>apply to something better
your 1 year at the warehouse shows an employer you have changed your life, show up to work on time, and aren't retarded enough to get fired in the first few weeks/months of a job. If you are still a neet at mommy's house and actually save your money you should have at least 10 grand at this point which is a good starting point and even people that make 6 figures don't just have a spare 10k laying around.
>get a certification for anything PHYSICAL.
this could be networking, becoming a barber, having a furniture moving company, landscaping business. whatever. as long as you are certified in something that isnt an excel fake email desk job.
>after getting experience in the field you are certified and your 5 figure portfolio, start a business if you truly want to scale.
if thats not your style then enjoy your new job you got from being certified but know that you will be there for a while so don't pick anything too back breaking. go to physical therapy. get a massage every once in a while.

61956662
>>61956595
All of this is good advice but also consider getting a degree preferably something technical if you don’t want to go the trade route

61956697
I got a job washing dishes and after 9 months I got a job cutting vegetables and shit

61956702
>>61956436
How is an online degree going to help OP who likely has a CS degree from an actual university?

61956707
>>61956702
Where did OP mention that? He likely has 0 degree and low money. Post-covid online degrees are normalized esp in tech

61956723
>>61956707
Because it's 4chan and most 4chan NEETs have degrees?
You don't go from applying to hundreds of minimum wage jobs and not getting a single interview to suddenly landing interviews and getting jobs just because you've done a degree
That's not how it works
You're just setting OP up to more disappointment

61956731
>>61956723
I dint give advice on applying for jobs because it depends on what he studies. But absolutely getting a degree and verified skills moves the ball for literally anyone. You’re saying self-defeating shit

61957085
>>61956364
There's no magical solution. Your question is shallow and pointless without giving us context. I assume:
>you are living with your parents
>you don't have a car (and probably no license)
>you are nearing 30 or even reached it already
>you don't have a degree
>you have a high school diploma but nothing remarkable, judging from the way you ask questions expecting everything to be chewed and spoonfed to you, and from your typos
>probably have some sort of a degeneracy that is time and money consuming
It would really help if you could write down your situation and not just vaguepost like a retard.
Also, you decided to get out of NEETdom literally at the worst possible time since the covid lockdowns. Good luck with that, but here are my tips:
First and foremost, you need to unfuck yourself. You need to get used to suffer for long hours. You need to get used to a situation where there's no light at the end of the tunnel. Laziness is fucking over - you wake up and it is all suffering until one hour before sleep. There's no comfort anymore in your world. Can't deal with this? Give up already and stay a decaying corpse.
After you are sure you are resilient and ready to make it, do the following:
>stay with your parents no matter what. you will have to still leech on them for the next several years if not decade
>get a job, even if it is the most basic shit, deliver food for all I care
>get a car license if you don't have one
>get a beater reliable car - rare these days but possible
>work on a degree that will be useful in the near future, try to aim for becoming a nurse because that's a guaranteed job forever
>get a job where you can save money
When you reach this point, make another post asking what's next.

61957118
>>61956364
personally I'd register an entity with SAM, search upcoming contracting opportunities on SAM, find a janitorial contract in my area on SAM, find out how much it currently pays by searching past awards on SAM, find the Contracting Officer on SAM, and then contact them with my entity name and email on SAM and tell them I'm interested in bidding.

Then I'd bid the job lower than it's currently being paid and when I got the job I'd go clean a government building 6 days per week for hundreds of dollars per hour.

then I'd repeat that process 5 or 6 more times and start hiring other losers to work for me cleaning government buildings for hundreds of dollars per hour.

Then I'd hire one more loser to watch all those losers, and then another loser to find jobs and bid them, and then I'd retire.

61957123
>>61956426
TRVKE

61957212
>>61956364
deport all indians

61957756
>>61956364
I'm not sure how I'd lie about a 14 year gap.

61957816
>>61956364

61957838
You have to put barriers between yourself and your enabler

If its your parents it might mean moving away and changing your number, if its your government then idk

61957974
>>61957085
>just live a horrible life with no way out for 10 years instead of just being a comfy neet

wagies are incredible

61958365
>>61957974
I used to be a NEET. Yes, live life horribly for a few years to guarantee stability and a future. If you can NEET until death then by all means go for it. I personally was running out of time, and I'm the type of person that wants to start a family so NEETing is not a real option (it could be an option if I was genetically blessed enough for a rich girl to keep me as a sex-toy stay-at-home husband, but alas my dick is only 6 inches and I'm a 6ft king of manlets)

61958461
>>61958365
>6ft king of manlets
Uhh anon... manlet cutoff was officially extended to 6'2" in January 2025...

61958498
>>61958365
fair enough, ive given up on the idea of a family, i think my kids would suffer terribly. just wanna find a way to not feel like such a bug. maybe part time and just coasting

61949441

61949447

61949448

61949452

61949468

Heard of eToro, fool!

61949479
>>61949468

61949484
Imma gonna short firefighting equipment, the market oversaturated with combustion.

61949489
>>61949388
I don't believe the hype. It's not 2001 anymore, the Middle East is not the de-facto world supplier of oil. Nowadays there are countless exporters of oil on the field. That's what I see, that's where my money is.
There will surely be real consequences for this debacle. It will not be the oil catastrophe it's being hyped up to be.

61949504
>>61949448
not your barrels not your oil

61949511
>>61949388
Did you short oil thinking Trump was going to TACO? Is that why you need us to join your retarded trade so that we can light our money on fire alongside you?

61949515
>>61949489
it's a global market. also isn't not as simple as oil = oil. there are different grades or types of oil. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_crude_oil_products refineries are built to process certain grades of oil. you can't easily re-tool a refinery to process other grades, it's a massive undertaking that costs a lot of money.

61949516
I put several grand into oil futures yesterday. Keep bombing all Persian Gulf production facilities pls.

61949589
>>61949440
ANTI-GRAVITATIONAL KNIFE

61949595
>>61949489
Gulf is 20% of world supply
Demand is highly inelastic

61949675
>>61949419
>Hello Europe, the price of oil has risen
>Hello Middle East, we definitely want the same amount of oil since we clearly have absurd amounts of money and aren't struggling at all

61949825
Trump will set pump prices. No more increases. Prices will fall soon.

61949895
This but unironically, the puts are dirt cheap, the otherside of the trade is filled with retards. It just takes one tweet

61949935
>>61949825
I think they will try this. Bessent said they’re going to announce measures Thursday that’ll possibly include shorting the futures.

61950453
OIL BROS, WHAT KIND OF BARRELS DO I BUY?!?!

61950522
>>61949895
This. Otm puts on everything was and somehow still is a bargain

61950803
>>61949388
Im underinvested in wti

61950809
>>61949437
Yeah that worked out real well this time lol. I told everyone I could to pay attention to Trump’s actions and not his words before he got back in office. His policy is to confuse and befuddle his opponents both domestic and abroad so they can’t stonewall him like they used to. Worked out great compared to that dumpster fire of a first term but for some reason a lot of people on the right liked the neocons, never ending migrant caravans and Russia investigations.

61953557
>>61949504

61957678
>>61949440
They're called rising knives you financially illiterate retard.

61957688
>>61949438
>>61949388
https://oilprice.com/oil-price-charts/

Don't short oil , it may go to 120-150.

61957789
>>61949675
So your saying europe will buy less oil meaning demand is lower? Msm has been putting out fud and people are panic buying though

61957863
>>61957789
>Msm has been putting out fud and people are panic buying though
I'm talking about effects which take longer than a week to materialize. If this war takes longer than the US initially expected which is fairly likely since they actually thought the Iranians would prefer the US over their religious leader Europe's economy will suffer so massively that the demand will lower.

item.title: How high will OIL go? — 40 replies — 8.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
How high will OIL go?

Open thread • Replies: 40 • Updated: 2026-03-09 12:45 UTC

OP

150?
200?

Early replies (first 3)

61950050
>>61949961
The Trump admin probably starts trying to implement price controls above $150.

61950059
>>61949961
10,000 Gold.

61950077
How will crypto perform without rate cuts in the next 3 years?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61950790
>>61950728
>If gas prices only doubled from their pre-war average of ~$2.90/gal we'd see ~$5.80/gal.
a literal nothingburger.

BUYING GAS IS THE CHEAPEST PART OF OWNING A CAR!

61950801
>>61950728
>$8.28/gallon.
I checked and its higher then that in Germany.

61950847
>>61950801
My math was for the US only. As the world's largest producer of oil and now an oil exporting country, we have it better than most other countries.

61950857
>>61949961
No one cares about oil prices anymore, chud.

61950868
>>61950790
Driving a little gasoline Toyota Corolla 100,000 miles @ 40mpg and $5.80/gal: $14,500

Driving a larger electric Toyota bZ4X 100,000 miles at 252 miles per charge and $15/charge: $5,952

Look at what happened to the motor vehicle market after the 1973 and 1979 oil crises. Definitely not a nothingburger for investors.

61950872
>>61950857
Didn't Tesla stop making cars?

61950875
>>61950872
>tesla is the only ev maker
>>61950868
>still not owning an ev

61951080
>>61950184
>non-working radars
Funny that you say that

61951096
>>61949961
What oil stock do I buy bros????

61951236
>>61951080
Here is a real image, unlike your lazy well poisoning attempt
https://edition.cnn.com/2026/03/05/middleeast/radar-bases-us-missile-defense-iran-war-intl-invs

61951283
>>61949961
What will happen on Monday bros..?

61951306
>>61951283
Oil go up

61951325
>>61949961
At this point, over 300. Short term I'm guessing around 150.

61951346
>>61949961
I still don't understand why Americans give a blood sacrifice to Israel every 10 years. Is it some sexual thing?

61951350
>>61949961

This is the biggest oil production cut in the history of mankind. I don’t think an initial spike to $300 before face melting deflation kicks in is out of the question

61951441
>>61951346
It is a sexual thing and it's on tape.

61952245
>>61950875
>still not owning an ev

In the 90s and 2000s my dad was 100% against cell phones, despite cell phones having been around in some form since the 70s. He grudgingly finally got one for business around 2010. Now he doesn't have a home landline. You'll huff and puff for another decade while the world moves on from internal combustion vehicles, but you'll eventually own one like everyone else. Every oil crisis has accelerated the replacement of less efficient vehicles with more efficient vehicles, and we've maxxed out the efficiency of gasoline engines. New battery technology is already doubling range and longevity while halving charge time over what was possible just a couple of years ago. People are running out of excuses to avoid EVs and reasons to keep ICEs. So, you can be the modern equivalent of a guy in the 1930s still riding a horse into town and rejecting those new fangled automobiles, but from an investor standpoint EVs and the technology surrounding them are where the growth is and will continue to be. Personally, I look forward to a world without jeet-owned gas stations and white trash greasemonkey youtube slop.

61952254
>>61951441

oh vey

61952315
>>61952245
Yeah you want overpriced golf carts with planned obsolescence instead. Such an improvement!

61952559
>>61950713
>>61950728
Based and rare high analysis anon, Im doing an investigation currently myself but it’s difficult without drawing from past source’s because the current MSM story is far removed from reality even from a cursory glance. Need to keep eyes on the situation as it unravels, but it seems you’re indicating that this is more than a short spike and could be a year long event?

61954746
>>61950868
Spend an extra $20,000 on a car to save $8548 on gasoline

yore a geneos dude

61955625
>>61949961
it was about -$40 a few years ago so I'm guessing it might reach $3.50 soon

61956311
>>61954746
The nice thing about gasoline cars is that you can "recharge" in less than 5 minutes AND have the ability to double your range simply by carrying extra gas cans.

Electric takes HOURS to charge, and you can't exactly take an extra battery with you.

It's a matter of convenience, and then there is the big brother IoT tracking your every move, which electric vehicles will surely have in them + hardwired kill switches. But that's getting a little off in the weeds.

Combustion engine vehicles are just simply better, more reliable, more convenient.

61956709
slurp the dip?

61957060
>>61952559
>but it seems you’re indicating that this is more than a short spike and could be a year long event?

1. Oil extraction is already shutting down or gas been shut down in the regions that ship oil through the strait.

2. Oil storage in these regions is maxxed out or about to be maxxed out.

3. It takes a couple of weeks to get production going again.

4. It takes a few weeks to queue up tankers, fill them, have them travel halfway around the world, and offload their oil.

5. It takes maybe a week to refine that offloaded oil and have it ready for distribution.

6. Countries like Japan are already tapping their strategic reserves, which will divert some of that oil.

So, if the war ends tomorrow and speculators leave the oil market, fuel prices will still remain elevated due to simple supply constraints for *at least* 6 to 8 weeks. Realistically, the war is going to last at least that long and the strategic reserves of various countries will need even more refilling. We're probably looking at 6 months of high gas prices *at least*.

7. Unlike 1973, the Saudis and a couple of other countries aren't cutting production but increasing it. So prices will likely stabilize at an elevated level soon(ish).

8. Unlike 1979, the US is now a net exporter of oil and oil extraction has increased around the world thanks to new technologies. There are many more vehicles in the world, but the global motor pool is also significantly more efficient than in 1979/80.

So, high fuel prices are going to last awhile, but probably won't get as high as they would if we were facing the exact same situation as in '73 or '79.

61957226
>>61956311
So you drive 300 miles to work or something?

61957526
>>61950050
Then the sellers will just stop shipping and production.
It will go over $200.

61957540
$250 and momentary spike to $800 at some point.

61957556
All markets at this point are skewed by massive retail gambling, you're going to get burned if you chase price action.

item.title: Certified Banger Money Farming — 16 replies — 8.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
Certified Banger Money Farming

Open thread • Replies: 16 • Updated: 2026-03-09 15:33 UTC

OP

What are some methods, from which you have actually seen returns from, to make money outside of a day job
Side hustles, beer money, whatever you wanna call it, just name some good grinds out there that you could recommend to basically anyone
>Uhhhh just invest or hedge against some shitcoin
Not the point of the thread fuck off!
Mostly online methods preferred but not necessary (Yes I am aware some things take physical effort)
If it can be done both on a phone and PC even better
Personally I have been trying to form my own hustle for a little over a month now and have a subscriber base totaling $100 a month, not bad but nothing major

Early replies (first 3)

61952894
>>61952873
Indoor shrimp farming + Garloid wrangling

item.content:encoded:
NO MARKET CRASH

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-03-09 14:54 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

61957985

61957994
>pro-China europoor post
>it's always a moortuguese or gayreek

61958009
>>61957935
China's insulation will help China, not otheres

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61958013
>>61957935
>Dude is living in China and posting non-stop every day how great China is.
Wow, we should listen to him. He sounds very unbiased.

61958031
>>61958013

61958063
>>61957994
They are desperate. Because since late 25 its not just about eating loses, its the question how they die. Suicide or somebody else making the decision for them

61958079
>>61958031
It is a fact that China is on a downward velocity and unlike western countries it is not one they can avoid.

61958082
Jiang said this would happen. Another jiang dub

61958142
>>61958013
>>61958079
>>61957994
>>61958063
MIGA

61958150
China unironically has been checkmated by Trump. By their own admission they are not yet ready for Taiwan and their do nothing strategy is getting all their "allies" btfo. They can either go now and lose or continue to do nothing and lose. Better luck next century chang

61958154
>>61958082
I like this Jiang guy when it comes to geopolitics and well, he has a really good track record. But what do you think about him when he goes full schizo?

61958160
>>61958150
China has been checkmated by itself 25 years ago, arguably 36 years ago. China survived barely by the stupidity of BoomerX who are as a generation of analogue easily manipulated scum irrelevant since late 25

61958207
>>61958142
>uh oh, they’re not gobbling up my propaganda
>better accuse them of being MAGA
China numbah wan

61958219
>>61958154
You gotta take the good with the bad. Plus, even the more outlandish ideas he talks about may have grain of truth to them
